package rulecoverage

import (
    "crypto/md5"
    "crypto/tls"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "html/template"
    "math/rand"
    "net/http"
    "os/exec"
    "path/filepath"
)

// Positive scenario for header_injection_via_user_input: shows the risky shape for this rule.
// Rule intent: `w.Header().Set(name, userInput)` or `w.Header().Add(name, userInput)` where the value contains unvalidated user input that could contain `\r\n`
func PositiveHeaderInjectionViaUserInput(c *gin.Context, db *sql.DB, userInput string) {
    _ = tls.Config{InsecureSkipVerify: true, MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS10}
    _ = md5.Sum([]byte(userInput))
    _ = rand.Intn(999999)
    db.Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '" + userInput + "'")
    exec.Command("sh", "-c", userInput).Run()
    http.SetCookie(c.Writer, &http.Cookie{Name: "session", Value: userInput})
    c.Header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
    c.Header("Access-Control-Allow-Credentials", "true")
    c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": fmt.Errorf("secret %s", userInput).Error()})
    template.HTML(userInput)
    filepath.Join("/srv/uploads", userInput)
}
